Patient Name : KATHLEEN WHARTON Admission Date : 03/24/2026 Birth Date : 06/23/1944 East Valley Rehabilitation Hospital MR # JX00003849 Visit #: JX0000048702 Patient ID : JX0000048702 Physical Therapy Daily Progress Note Treatment date/time/type Treatment date 04/06/2026 Treatment 1 time 09:30 10:30 Total therapy minutes 60 Individual therapy minutes 60 Concurrent therapy minutes 0 Group therapy minutes 0 Co-treatment therapy minutes 0 Encounter Encounter details Dx: L2 wedge fracture due to a GLF Prec: goes by "Katie", fall risk, stroke in 12/25, not able to move RUE due to stroke, R AFO Subjective Subjective Pt greeted supine in bed and agreeable to PT with no reports of pain/dizziness at the start of session. Quality Indicator: Roll left and right Roll left and right Roll left and right details Helper provided the following assistance to roll: Assistance with LESS than or exactly half of all rolling tasks, including: Rolling to right from back, Rolling to left from back Roll left and right device(s) No device Comments Patient is independent rolling R, min assist rolling L secondary to residual weakness/hemiparesis R side from CVA 12-2025. Roll left and right: Current Status Partial/moderate assistance - 3 Roll left and right: Goal Independent - 6 Quality Indicator: Sit to lying Sit to lying Sit to lying details Helper provided the following assistance to move from sitting to lying: Verbal cues, Contact guard assistance Sit to lying device(s) No device Comments Patient is CGA for BTB for upper/lower quarter, VC/TC for R UE/LE, hemi-technique due to residual weakness from CVA UE>LE. Sit to lying: Current Status Touching assistance - 4 Sit to lying: Goal Independent - 6 Quality Indicator: Lying to sitting on side of bed Lying to sitting on side of bed Lying to sitting on side of bed details Helper provided the following assistance to move from lying to sitting: Setting up device(s), Verbal cues, Contact guard assistance Lying to sitting on side of bed device(s) Bed rails Comments Patient has an adjustable bed at home, CGA OOB L side with flat bed. Lying to sitting on side of bed: Current Touching assistance - 4 Status Printed by Cortney Bounsinh, 4/8/2026 8:59:34 AM Page 1 of 15 Document Name : Physical Therapy Daily Progress Note (v1.7) Dignity Health. Sit to stand: Current Status Partial/moderate assistance - 3 Sit to stand: Goal Touching assistance - 4 Quality Indicator:Chair/bed-to-chair transfer Chair/bed-to-chair transfer Chair/bed-to-chair transfer details Helper provided the following assistance to transfer to and from a bed to chair (or wheelchair): Assistance with LESS than or exactly half of all transfer tasks, including: Trunk support Chair/bed-to-chair transfer device(s) HW Comments Patient is min assist for SPT from variety of surfaces using HW, cues for R LE foot/alignment/positioning, weight-shifting, hand placement on WC and correct placement of HW, pivoting, turning, BOS. Chair/bed-to-chair transfer: Current Status Partial/moderate assistance - 3 Chair/bed-to-chair transfer: Goal Touching assistance - 4 Quality Indicator: Car transfer Car transfer Car transfer details Helper provided the following assistance to transfer in and out of car or van: Setting up device(s), Verbal cues, Assistance with LESS than or exactly half of all transfer tasks, including: Trunk support, Pivoting, lifting R LE Car transfer device(s) leg lifter / HW Comments Patient is min assist for car transfers using HW, cues for R LE foot/alignment/positioning, weight-shifting, hand placement on WC and correct placement of HW, pivoting, turning, BOS. Car transfer: Current Status Partial/moderate assistance - 3 Car transfer: Goal Touching assistance - 4 Quality Indicator: Walk 10 feet Walk 10 feet Walk 10 feet details Helper provided the following assistance for patient to walk 10 feet: Setting up device(s), Verbal cues, Contact guard assistance Walk 10 feet device(s) HW Comments Patient is able to ambulate X 10 feet level surfaces using HW with CGA, VC/TC for BOS, sequencing, HW placement, performs step 2 patterning. Patient limited to 6 stairs secondary to LE fatigu R>L. 12 steps: Current Status Not attempted due to medical condition or safety concerns - 88 12 steps: Goal Partial/moderate assistance - 3 Quality Indicator: Wheelchair Does the patient use a wheelchair and/or No scooter? Assessment/Plan Patient's response to treatment Assessed Weekly Qls with CGA/Min A d/t instability despite using HW for stability on all mobility tasks. Pt reports increased fatigue post gait able to amb 150ft today. Progress towards goals Progressing Communication with other disciplines PT Details of interdisciplinary communication Supervision by Tess Hofstra PT, DPT and Pt appropriate for PTA to provide treatment. Printed by Cortney Bounsinh, 4/8/2026 8:59:34 AM Page 4 of 15 Document Name : Physical Therapy Daily Progress Note (v1.7) Dignity Health. Pt remains sitting up in WC, seat alarm in place, call light/telephone in reach, and bedside table in front of pt. Pt reports this writer meets all their needs. Pt not agreeable to engage in therapeutic interventions and stated: "I'm too depressed to do anything, I just don't want to be here anymore." Pt also stated: "I walked around a lot with (PT) today, I don't need to do anymore." Toilet transfer: Current Status Patient refused - 7 Education Education provided in patient's preferred English language Education provided Benefits of therapy Method of education Verbal instruction Education provided to Patient Response of patient or caregiver Needs reinforcement, continued education, or both Education plan Therapist used good therapeutic use of self and empathetic listening as pt voiced concerns of overall depression. Therapist provided max. education and encouragement to attempt to engage in functional ADL tasks and leisure activities to improve pts participation in functional tasks necessary to maximize independence and improve overall QOL; however, pt declined all attempted interventions despite therapists max. encouragement and education on benefits and importance of participating. Assessment/Plan Patient's response to treatment Pt not agreeable to engage in therapeutic interventions; therefore, tx session ended early on this date. Therapist will attempt further therapeutic interventions next tx session. Attempted tx session took place in pts room. Pt left in comfortable position side-lying in bed w/ HOB raised, w/ all needs met and call light and tray table in reach. Communication with other disciplines Case management Details of interdisciplinary communication Pt perseverating on d/c plans throughout attempted tx session and therapist collaborated w/ CM. CM stated would follow up w/ pt. Plan Continue oT POC.
